nameMeanings.name: Drogon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Emile, Adelard, Alphonse, Andre, Antoine, Jean Robert, Marcel, Michel.
Americanized form of French Daragon (see Deragon ).

French and English: nickname perhaps for a fierce or fiery person or occupational name for someone who carried a standard in battle or else in a pageant or procession, from Old French (Middle English) drago(u)n ‘snake, serpent, water monster’ (from Latin draco, genitive draconis, from Greek drakōn, ultimately from derkesthai ‘to flash’). This word was applied in Late Latin to military standards in the form of windsocks, so resembling snakes.

variants.name: Drakos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Greek Antonios, Christos, Diamantis, Fotios, Konstantinos, Kostas.

Greek: from the personal name Drakos, from drakos ‘dragon, ogre’ (classical Greek drakōn, ultimately from derkesthai ‘to flash’), an omen or well-wishing name expressing the wish that the boy so named will grow up to be as strong as an ogre. Compare Dragon .

variants.name: Deragon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Monique, Pierre, Raoul, Rejean.

French: variant of Daragon, habitational name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, denoting someone from Aragon, a region and medieval kingdom in Spain (see Aragon ), or a habitational name for someone from Aragon, the name of several places in various parts of France. The surname Deragon is very rare in France. Compare Dragon .

variants.name: Dragan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Alexandru, Florin, Mihaela, Radu, Sorin, Stelian.  Polish Ewa, Halina, Janusz, Zosia.
Romanian, Serbian, Croatian, and Slovenian: from the South Slavic personal name Dragan, derived from drag ‘dear, beloved’ or from a compound name based on this element, e.g. Dragoslav.
Polish and Ukrainian: nickname from dragan (later also dragon) ‘dragoon’. Compare Dragon .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Boves
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Czech (Boreš): from a pet form of any of various compound personal names, formed with the Slavic element bor ‘to fight’ (see Borak ).
German (also Böres): variant of Börries and Borries .

variants.name: Borak
meanings: Polish, Croatian, and Serbian: from a pet form of any of various Slavic compound personal names, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic and South Slavic boriti se), e.g. Polish Borzysław, Croatian and Serbian Borislav (see also Boris ).

Czech and Slovak (Borák): habitational name for someone from any of many places called with bor ‘pine forest’; alternatively, from a pet form of any of various Slavic compound personal names, containing the element bor ‘to fight’, e.g. Czech Dalibor (compare 1 above).
Slovak (Borák): nickname or topographic name from the plant name borák ‘borage’.

nameMeanings.name: Dreux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Channel Islands:  See  Druce  .

variants.name: Druce
meanings: Transferred use of the Scottish surname, now used as a given name throughout the English-speaking world. In the 20th century it was particularly popular in Australia. The surname was originally a Norman baronial name, but a precise identification of the place from which it was derived has not been made (there are a large number of possible candidates). The Bruces were an influential Norman family in Scottish affairs in the early Middle Ages; its most famous member was  Robert  ‘the Bruce’ (  1274–1329 ), who is said to have drawn inspiration after his defeat at Methven from the perseverance of a spider in repeatedly climbing up again after being knocked down. He ruled Scotland as  King Robert I  from  1306 to 1329 .

en.lifeSummary: When Dreux Drogon de Boves was born in 0998, in Boves, Somme, Picardie, France, his father, Hugues DE BOVES D'ACHEY-DE FERRIERES, was 23 and his mother, Avoise d'Aigle, was 15. He had at least 2 daughters with Adèle DE CREPY. He died in 1077, in his hometown, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dreux Drogon de Boves est né en 0998, à Boves, Somme, Picardie, France, son père, Hugues DE BOVES D'ACHEY-DE FERRIERES, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Avoise d'Aigle, avait 15 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Adèle DE CREPY. Il est décédé en 1077, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Dreux Drogon de Boves 0998 in Boves, Somme, Picardie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugues DE BOVES D'ACHEY-DE FERRIERES 23 und seine Mutter Avoise d'Aigle 15. Er hatte mit Adèle DE CREPY mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb 1077 im Alter von 79 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Dreux Drogon de Boves è nato il nel 0998 nel Boves, Somme, Picardie, Francia, il suo padre, Hugues DE BOVES D'ACHEY-DE FERRIERES, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Avoise d'Aigle, aveva 15 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Adèle DE CREPY. Morì nel 1077, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Dreux Drogon de Bovesが0998年にフランスピカーディソンメボベスで生まれた時，父Hugues DE BOVES D'ACHEY-DE FERRIERESは23歳，母Avoise d'Aigleは15歳であった。彼はAdèle DE CREPYとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1077年に故郷で79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Dreux Drogon de Boves(이)가 0998년 프랑스 피카디 솜 보브스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugues DE BOVES D'ACHEY-DE FERRIERES(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Avoise d'Aigle(은)는 15세였다. 그는 Adèle DE CREPY(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1077년 그의 고향에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Dreux Drogon de Boves nasceu em 0998, em Boves, Somme, Picardie, França, seu pai, Hugues DE BOVES D'ACHEY-DE FERRIERES, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Avoise d'Aigle, tinha 15 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Adèle DE CREPY. Ele faleceu em 1077, em sua cidade natal, com 79 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dreux Drogon de Boves родился в 0998 г. в местности бовесь, сомм, пикарди, Франция, его отцу по имени Hugues DE BOVES D'ACHEY-DE FERRIERES было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Avoise d'Aigle, 15. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Adèle DE CREPY. Он умер в 1077 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Dreux Drogon de Boves nació en 0998, en Boves, Somme, Picardie, Francia, su papá, Hugues DE BOVES D'ACHEY-DE FERRIERES, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Avoise d'Aigle, tenía 15 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Adèle DE CREPY. Murió en 1077, en su pueblo, a la edad de 79 años.
